mechanism_narrative: An increase in FEDFUNDS over the prior 3 months raises equity discount rates and tightens financing conditions, causing lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when policy is in a tightening phase.
evidence_refs: fact:Federal Reserve tightening cycle was observable before 2006-08-15
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month changes in FEDFUNDS are followed by positive or unchanged 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward observations during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals weakening housing demand, reduced construction activity, and deteriorating credit-sensitive household balance sheets, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% year over year
evidence_refs: fact:Housing activity was a visible macro risk before 2006-08-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST declines are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the triggered mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage point over 3 months signals labor-market deterioration and rising recession probability, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases do not produce lower average 20-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered observations, or if the downside hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is accelerating while FEDFUNDS is already high, equity investors price additional tightening and lower valuation multiples, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth rises and FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL acceleration and high FEDFUNDS condition has no more negative 20-day return distribution than either single-series condition alone.

mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in RSAFS indicates resilient household demand and stronger expected corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth is not associated with higher subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward tests or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month growth in INDPRO signals stronger factory output and operating leverage for cyclical firms, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO growth crossing above zero is followed by negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if the signal underperforms the unconditional 60-day return mean.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth slows while UNRATE rises, labor income risk increases and expected consumption weakens, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprice growth risk.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth decelerates and UNRATE rises over the same 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS slowdown and UNRATE rise does not generate lower 5-day S&P 500 returns than PAYEMS slowdown alone or UNRATE rise alone.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDPC1 growth combined with stable or falling FEDFUNDS indicates real demand expansion without an immediate discount-rate shock, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 year-over-year growth exceeds 2% and FEDFUNDS does not rise over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of strong GDPC1 and non-rising FEDFUNDS fails to deliver positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns or performs no better than GDPC1 alone.
